trivia_bullets.text: Ghostly Sentinel's type line is 'Creature — Kor Spirit', making it a spiritual ancestor of the kor race rather than a generic ghost. The Stone Havens referenced in the flavor text are the canonical kor sacred refuge-settlements on Zendikar — cliff-carved fastnesses where the kor preserve their history and call on ancestral dead for protection during the Eldrazi siege. First and only printing.

"Mystics of the Stone Havens call upon the spirits of fallen heroes to defend the refuges." Frames the card as a conjured kor ancestor — the Stone Havens' mystics perform ancestral-spirit summoning to defend resistance refuges from the Eldrazi.

A 3/3 with flying and vigilance for {4}{W}; pauper-legal and commander-legal. EDHREC rank 29,001 is near floor — a Limited-format card only. Vigilance mechanically encodes the flavor: an ever-watchful sentinel that never drops its guard, even when attacking.

Art by Daarken (Mike Lim), who has illustrated 200+ Magic cards since 2007. His Hipsters of the Coast 'Behind the Brush' profile (2019) notes his consistently dark-toned aesthetic and his transition from D&D work to Magic illustration. No Zendikar-specific artist commentary surfaced in the Hipsters interview.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast, 'Behind the Brush—Daarken,' 2019
